title: [ETC] Extract a parent array instead of individual array access
body:
Consider following IR:
```mlir
hw.module @Array(%clock: i1, %cond: i1, %array: !hw.array<4xi1>) -> () {
    %w = sv.wire : !hw.inout<!hw.array<4xi1>>
    sv.assign %w, %array : !hw.array<4xi1>
    %c0 = hw.constant 0: i2
    %c1 = hw.constant 1: i2
    %c2 = hw.constant 2: i2
    %c3 = hw.constant 3: i2
    %w1 = sv.array_index_inout %w[%c0] : !hw.inout<!hw.array<4xi1>>, i2
    %w2 = sv.array_index_inout %w[%c1] : !hw.inout<!hw.array<4xi1>>, i2
    %w3 = sv.array_index_inout %w[%c2] : !hw.inout<!hw.array<4xi1>>, i2
    %w4 = sv.array_index_inout %w[%c3] : !hw.inout<!hw.array<4xi1>>, i2
    %cond1 = sv.read_inout %w1 : !hw.inout<i1>
    %cond2 = sv.read_inout %w2 : !hw.inout<i1>
    %cond3 = sv.read_inout %w3 : !hw.inout<i1>
    %cond4 = sv.read_inout %w4 : !hw.inout<i1>
    sv.always posedge %clock  {
      sv.cover %cond1, immediate
      sv.cover %cond2, immediate
      sv.cover %cond3, immediate
      sv.cover %cond4, immediate
    }
  }
```
The current firtool output with ETC:
```verilog
module Array_cover(	// foo.mlir:1:1
  input _GEN,
        _GEN_0,
        _GEN_1,
        _GEN_2,
        clock);

  always @(posedge clock) begin	// foo.mlir:16:5
    cover(_GEN);	// foo.mlir:17:7
    cover(_GEN_0);	// foo.mlir:18:7
    cover(_GEN_1);	// foo.mlir:19:7
    cover(_GEN_2);	// foo.mlir:20:7
  end // always @(posedge)
endmodule

module Array(	// foo.mlir:1:1
  input       clock,
              cond,
  input [3:0] array);

  wire       _Array_cover_;	// foo.mlir:1:1
  wire       _Array_cover__0;	// foo.mlir:1:1
  wire       _Array_cover__1;	// foo.mlir:1:1
  wire       _Array_cover__2;	// foo.mlir:1:1
  wire [3:0] w;	// foo.mlir:2:10
  assign w = array;	// foo.mlir:3:5
  assign _Array_cover_ = w[2'h0];	// foo.mlir:1:1, :4:11, :8:11, :12:14
  assign _Array_cover__0 = w[2'h1];	// foo.mlir:1:1, :5:11, :9:11, :13:14
  assign _Array_cover__1 = w[2'h2];	// foo.mlir:1:1, :6:11, :10:11, :14:14
  assign _Array_cover__2 = w[2'h3];	// foo.mlir:1:1, :7:11, :11:11, :15:14
  /* This instance is elsewhere emitted as a bind statement.
    Array_cover Array_cover (	// foo.mlir:1:1
      ._GEN      (_Array_cover_),	// foo.mlir:1:1
      ._GEN_0      (_Array_cover__0),	// foo.mlir:1:1
      ._GEN_1      (_Array_cover__1),	// foo.mlir:1:1
      ._GEN_2      (_Array_cover__2),	// foo.mlir:1:1
      .clock (clock)
    );
  */
endmodule
```

Extracting individual elements increases the number of ports. Instead, we might want to extract an entire array and access individual elements, e.g:
```verilog
module Array_cover(	// foo.mlir:1:1
  input w,
        clock);

  always @(posedge clock) begin	// foo.mlir:16:5
    cover(w[0]);	// foo.mlir:17:7
    cover(w[1]);	// foo.mlir:18:7
    cover(w[2]);	// foo.mlir:19:7
    cover(w[3]);	// foo.mlir:20:7
  end // always @(posedge)
endmodule

module Array(	// foo.mlir:1:1
  input       clock,
              cond,
  input [3:0] array);

  wire [3:0] w;	// foo.mlir:2:10
  assign w = array;	// foo.mlir:3:5
  /* This instance is elsewhere emitted as a bind statement.
    Array_cover Array_cover (	// foo.mlir:1:1
      .w      (w),	// foo.mlir:1:1
      .clock (clock)
    );
  */
endmodule
```
